candidate_pool.rationale: The backward formula (1 - expf(t)) * d where t is the forward output is one of several valid gradient expressions for log_sigmoid. Alternatives include sigmoid(-x)*d, expf(-x)/(1+expf(-x))*d, or (logistic(-x))*d — each is mathematically equivalent but produces different numerical behavior at extreme values. Not recoverable from sibling ops because each op has a unique gradient formula.
candidate_pool.patch_evidence: return (1 - expf(t)) * d in scalar_log_sigmoid_backward_op operator()

candidate_pool.rationale: The Eigen functor_traits Cost for the forward op is AddCost*6 + MulCost*4, a non-trivial value specific to this op. Sibling ops have different cost values. Wrong cost values cause Eigen to make suboptimal vectorization decisions. The answer space is large (any combination of AddCost/MulCost coefficients).
candidate_pool.patch_evidence: Cost = NumTraits<Scalar>::AddCost *6 + NumTraits<Scalar>::MulCost * 4 in scalar_log_sigmoid_forward_op functor_traits

candidate_pool.rationale: The forward packetOp uses a non-obvious branchless min/max pivot strategy with a constant of -1.0 to implement the piecewise computation without SIMD branching. No sibling functor uses this technique. Alternatives include mask-based blending, sequential scalar fallback, or polynomial approximation — each produces different performance and precision characteristics. Independent from ambiguous_stable_math (which covers the scalar formula choice, not the SIMD algorithm).
candidate_pool.patch_evidence: pmin(padd(pmin(minus_one, psub(x, plog1p(pexp(x)))), pmax(minus_one, pnegate(plog1p(pexp(pnegate(x)))))), minus_one) in scalar_log_sigmoid_forward_op packetOp

candidate_pool.rationale: The backward uses binaryExpr (two-input Eigen functor on fx and dEdf) but the function is defined as a unary operation, implying a unary Eigen functor in the backward. These are mutually exclusive in Eigen's template system. No sibling convention resolves this because sibling backward passes mix both unaryExpr and binaryExpr patterns without an observable rule.
The backward pass uses the cached forward output (fx) rather than recomputing from xs[0].
candidate_pool.rejection_reason: Not independent from backward_gradient_formula (whose resolution mentions the forward output) and backward_unary_vs_binary_functor (whose answer reveals the backward accesses fx). Dropped due to dependence.

candidate_pool.rationale: The forward functor_traits requires specific PacketAccess trait checks (HasLog1p, HasExp, HasNegate, HasAdd, HasSub, HasMin, HasMax).
candidate_pool.rejection_reason: Overlaps with forward_functor_traits_cost (both target the same functor_traits specialization block). Selecting cost instead.

candidate_pool.rationale: The backward functor_traits Cost is AddCost + 2*MulCost.
candidate_pool.patch_evidence: Cost = NumTraits<Scalar>::AddCost + 2 * NumTraits<Scalar>::MulCost in backward functor_traits
candidate_pool.rejection_reason: Overlaps with forward_functor_traits_cost (both target functor_traits Cost for the same operation). Independence concern.

candidate_pool.rationale: The nt::logsigmoid enum value in sig.h could be log_sigmoid, logsigmoid, log_sig, etc.
candidate_pool.rejection_reason: The agent can grep sig.h for the enum pattern and pick a valid name. Adjacent to rejected multibatch-support-conflict pattern.

ground_truth_annotation.omitted_element: The exact backward gradient formula and whether it is derived from the forward output or the raw input.
ground_truth_annotation.why_blocker: Multiple mathematically equivalent gradient formulas exist for log_sigmoid (sigmoid(-x)*d, (1-exp(t))*d, exp(-x)/(1+exp(-x))*d) and each produces different numerical behavior at extreme values. No sibling op shares this gradient, so copying is impossible.

independence.decision_point: Define the exact mathematical formula for the log_sigmoid backward gradient computation.

ground_truth_annotation.omitted_element: The exact Eigen functor_traits Cost coefficients (AddCost multiplier and MulCost multiplier) for the forward functor.
ground_truth_annotation.why_blocker: No universal default exists. Each mathematical functor has a unique cost profile depending on its internal operations. Sibling ops use different coefficient combinations (e.g. logistic uses different values than exp or log). Wrong coefficients cause Eigen to make suboptimal or incorrect vectorization scheduling decisions.

independence.decision_point: Define the exact Eigen functor_traits Cost coefficients for the forward log_sigmoid SIMD functor.

ground_truth_annotation.omitted_element: The branchless SIMD vectorization algorithm for the piecewise forward computation.
ground_truth_annotation.why_blocker: No universal default or sibling pattern exists. Standard approaches include mask-based blending (pand/pandn/por), select instructions, polynomial approximation, or the min/max pivot trick. Each produces different precision and performance characteristics. No other dynet functor uses the min/max pivot technique.

independence.decision_point: Define the branchless SIMD vectorization algorithm for the piecewise forward computation in the Eigen packetOp.

requirement_a.text: The operation is a unary function (one input, one output) and the backward Eigen functor should follow the same single-input pattern as other unary node backward passes that use unaryExpr.

requirement_b.text: The backward computation must reuse the cached forward output alongside the upstream gradient for correct and efficient gradient computation, requiring a two-input Eigen functor.

ground_truth_annotation.why_impossible: Eigen's unaryExpr takes exactly one tensor argument and cannot access a second tensor. Eigen's binaryExpr takes exactly two tensor arguments. There is no Eigen expression template that is simultaneously unary and binary. The backward pass cannot use unaryExpr if it needs both the forward output and the upstream gradient as functor inputs.

independence.decision_point: Resolve whether the backward pass uses a unary Eigen functor operating on the upstream gradient alone or a binary Eigen functor operating on two tensors.

rejected_blockers.attempted_decision_point: Define which tensor the backward gradient computation depends on: the cached forward output or the raw input.
rejected_blockers.failure_reason: Not independent from missing_backward_gradient_formula (whose resolution mentions the forward output) and contradictory_backward_functor_arity (whose answer reveals the backward accesses fx via binaryExpr). Solving either reveals this blocker's answer.
rejected_blockers.avoid_in_future: Do not create a separate blocker for backward tensor source when the gradient formula or functor arity blockers already reveal whether the backward uses the forward output.

rejected_blockers.attempted_decision_point: Specify which PacketAccess trait checks the forward functor_traits must declare.
rejected_blockers.failure_reason: Overlaps with missing_forward_eigen_cost — both target the same functor_traits specialization block for scalar_log_sigmoid_forward_op. Solving one reveals the other's location and structure.
rejected_blockers.avoid_in_future: Do not target multiple aspects of the same Eigen functor_traits specialization block as separate blockers.

rejected_blockers.attempted_decision_point: Specify the backward functor_traits Cost coefficients.
rejected_blockers.failure_reason: Overlaps with missing_forward_eigen_cost — both target functor_traits Cost for functors in the same file. Solving one reveals the pattern for the other.
rejected_blockers.avoid_in_future: Do not create separate cost-coefficient blockers for forward and backward functors of the same operation.

batch_audit.distribution_notes: Regenerated 3 missing_parameter and 1 contradictory_requirement to replace the 4 rejected round-1 blockers. All target numerical/behavioral/SIMD decisions in the log_sigmoid implementation that are NOT recoverable from sibling ops.
batch_audit.independence_summary: The 4 new blockers target distinct decisions: (1) backward gradient formula (what math to compute), (2) forward Eigen cost coefficients (optimizer hints), (3) SIMD branchless pivot algorithm (how to vectorize), (4) backward functor arity (unary vs binary Eigen pattern). None overlaps with frozen ambiguous_stable_math (which covers scalar negative-input formulation). No pair shares a decision point. Blockers 1 and 4 both involve the backward pass but are independent: knowing the formula does not tell you the Eigen template pattern, and knowing the arity does not tell you the formula.
batch_audit.candidate_pool_summary: 8 candidates generated (2x the 4-blocker deficit). 4 selected, 4 discarded. Sources: 8 golden_patch_reverse.

rejection_memory.attempted_decision_point: Define the exact input parameter type for the log_sigmoid function.
rejection_memory.failure_reason: The resolution (const Expression& x) is trivially recoverable from every sibling unary op in dynet/expr.cc and dynet/expr.h. Answer space is effectively size 1.
rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not target parameter type or signature elements that are identical across all sibling unary ops in the same codebase.

rejection_memory.attempted_decision_point: Identify which Python binding files must be updated to expose the new operation.
rejection_memory.failure_reason: The target files (_dynet.pxd, _dynet.pyx) are recoverable by grepping python/ and copying sibling bindings. Also borderline gotcha: task-scope plumbing, not a behavioral decision.
rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not target which files to update when the file set is discoverable by grepping existing sibling implementations in the same directory.

rejection_memory.attempted_decision_point: Identify the exact test file and test case name to add for verifying the operation's gradient.
rejection_memory.failure_reason: The test file and naming convention (X_gradient in tests/test-nodes.cc) are trivially recoverable from sibling test cases. The test name is not load-bearing for correct behavior.
rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not target test file names or test case names that follow an obvious naming convention visible in sibling tests.

rejection_memory.attempted_decision_point: Resolve whether the node supports multibatch operations.
rejection_memory.failure_reason: The contradiction (support batching vs disable multibatch) is artificial. Resolution (supports_multibatch() == true) is recoverable from every batchable sibling node which sets it true.
rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not create contradictions whose resolution is a boolean copied from all sibling nodes. Target decisions with genuinely large answer spaces that differ per-op.

rejection_memory.attempted_decision_point: Define which tensor the backward gradient computation depends on: the cached forward output or the raw input.
rejection_memory.failure_reason: Not independent from the backward gradient formula blocker and the backward functor arity blocker. Knowing the gradient formula (which mentions the forward output) reveals this blocker's answer. Knowing the functor arity (binary on fx and dEdf) also reveals it.
rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not create a separate blocker for backward tensor source when the gradient formula or functor arity blockers already reveal whether the backward uses the forward output.

rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not create a separate blocker for backward tensor source when the gradient formula or functor arity blockers already reveal whether the backward uses the forward output.

rejection_memory.avoid_in_future: Do not target multiple aspects of the same Eigen functor_traits specialization block as separate blockers.
